@@ -829,7 +829,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-33vh.5","title":"Remove acfs/claude/hooks/ directory references from deployed assets","description":"## Task Overview

Ensure the `acfs/claude/hooks/` directory structure is not created or referenced anywhere, as it was only used by the old git_safety_guard system.

## Current State

The acfs/claude/ directory currently contains only:
```
acfs/claude/settings.json  # Contains only schema + DCG comment
```

The `acfs/claude/hooks/` directory and its contents were removed in commit f1fd501.

## Verification Needed

### 1. Check for mkdir commands creating hooks directory
```bash
grep -rn 'mkdir.*claude.*hooks\|claude/hooks' --include='*.sh' .
grep -rn 'mkdir.*claude.*hooks\|claude/hooks' --include='*.py' .
```
Expected: No matches

### 2. Check for cp/mv commands referencing hooks directory
```bash
grep -rn 'cp.*claude.*hooks\|mv.*claude.*hooks' --include='*.sh' .
```
Expected: No matches

### 3. Check install.sh doesn't create this structure
Review install.sh for any:
- `mkdir -p $TARGET_HOME/.acfs/claude/hooks`
- Asset installation to hooks directory

## Why This Matters

The old doctor.sh check suggested:
```
Fix: mkdir -p ~/.claude/hooks && cp ~/.acfs/claude/hooks/git_safety_guard.py ~/.claude/hooks/
```

This path should not exist because:
1. DCG installs its own hook via `dcg install`
2. DCG uses a different mechanism (direct command, not Python file)
3. The source file doesn't exist anymore

## Action Items

1. Verify no code creates `~/.acfs/claude/hooks/`
2. Verify no code references `~/.acfs/claude/hooks/`
3. If any references found, remove them
4. Consider adding a .gitkeep or explicit documentation that this directory is intentionally absent

## Notes

- acfs/claude/settings.json should remain (it's a template)
- The settings.json comment already references DCG correctly:
  `"": "ACFS Claude Code base settings. DCG hooks are registered separately via 'dcg install'."`","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":15,"created_at":"2026-01-24T20:38:17.902849901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T20:38:17.902849901Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cleanup","file-structure"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33vh.5","depends_on_id":"bd-33vh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-24T20:38:17.902849901Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-33vh.6","title":"Create comprehensive grep audit script to verify complete removal","description":"## Task Overview

Create a verification script that can be run to confirm ALL git_safety_guard references have been removed from the codebase.

## Script Requirements

Create `scripts/tests/audit_git_safety_guard_removal.sh`:

```bash
#\!/usr/bin/env bash
# Audit script to verify complete removal of git_safety_guard
# Run from repo root: ./scripts/tests/audit_git_safety_guard_removal.sh

set -euo pipefail

REPO_ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"
cd "$REPO_ROOT"

echo "=== Auditing git_safety_guard Removal ==="
echo ""

FOUND=0

# Check 1: Literal string matches
echo "[1/5] Checking for 'git_safety_guard' literal string..."
if grep -rn 'git_safety_guard' --include='*.sh' --include='*.py' --include='*.ts' --include='*.tsx' --include='*.md' --include='*.yaml' --include='*.json' . 2>/dev/null | grep -v '.beads/' | grep -v 'audit_git_safety_guard'; then
    echo "❌ FOUND: git_safety_guard references"
    FOUND=1
else
    echo "✓ Clean: No git_safety_guard literals found"
fi

# Check 2: 'Git Safety Guard' (display name)
echo ""
echo "[2/5] Checking for 'Git Safety Guard' display name..."
if grep -rni 'Git Safety Guard' --include='*.sh' --include='*.py' --include='*.ts' --include='*.tsx' --include='*.md' . 2>/dev/null | grep -v '.beads/'; then
    echo "❌ FOUND: Git Safety Guard references"
    FOUND=1
else
    echo "✓ Clean: No 'Git Safety Guard' found"
fi

# Check 3: Python hook file
echo ""
echo "[3/5] Checking for git_safety_guard.py file..."
if find . -name 'git_safety_guard.py' -not -path './.beads/*' 2>/dev/null | grep .; then
    echo "❌ FOUND: git_safety_guard.py file exists"
    FOUND=1
else
    echo "✓ Clean: No git_safety_guard.py file"
fi

# Check 4: Hooks directory
echo ""
echo "[4/5] Checking for acfs/claude/hooks/ directory..."
if [[ -d "acfs/claude/hooks" ]]; then
    echo "❌ FOUND: acfs/claude/hooks/ directory exists"
    FOUND=1
else
    echo "✓ Clean: No acfs/claude/hooks/ directory"
fi

# Check 5: Doctor check function
echo ""
echo "[5/5] Checking doctor.sh for git_safety references..."
if grep -n 'safety_guard\|Git safety' scripts/lib/doctor.sh 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "❌ FOUND: Doctor still checks for git_safety"
    FOUND=1
else
    echo "✓ Clean: Doctor.sh has no git_safety references"
fi

echo ""
echo "=== Audit Complete ==="

if [[ $FOUND -eq 0 ]]; then
    echo "✅ All checks passed - git_safety_guard completely removed"
    exit 0
else
    echo "❌ Some checks failed - cleanup needed"
    exit 1
fi
```

## Usage

```bash
# Run from repo root
./scripts/tests/audit_git_safety_guard_removal.sh
```

## Expected Output (After Cleanup)

```
=== Auditing git_safety_guard Removal ===

[1/5] Checking for 'git_safety_guard' literal string...
✓ Clean: No git_safety_guard literals found

[2/5] Checking for 'Git Safety Guard' display name...
✓ Clean: No 'Git Safety Guard' found

[3/5] Checking for git_safety_guard.py file...
✓ Clean: No git_safety_guard.py file

[4/5] Checking for acfs/claude/hooks/ directory...
✓ Clean: No acfs/claude/hooks/ directory

[5/5] Checking doctor.sh for git_safety references...
✓ Clean: Doctor.sh has no git_safety references

=== Audit Complete ===
✅ All checks passed - git_safety_guard completely removed
```

## CI Integration

Consider adding to CI pipeline:
```yaml
# In .github/workflows/installer.yml
- name: Audit git_safety_guard removal
  run: ./scripts/tests/audit_git_safety_guard_removal.sh
```

## Notes

- Excludes .beads/ directory (historical issue tracking)
- Excludes the audit script itself
- Should be run after all cleanup tasks complete
- Exit code 0 = clean, 1 = found references","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":20,"created_at":"2026-01-24T20:38:39.747733599Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T20:39:55.137970745Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["qa","testing","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33vh.6","depends_on_id":"bd-33vh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-24T20:38:39.747733599Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-33vh.6","depends_on_id":"bd-33vh.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-24T20:39:52.476645914Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-33vh.6","depends_on_id":"bd-33vh.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-24T20:39:55.137904030Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-33vh.7","title":"Verify README.md only references DCG (no git_safety_guard)","description":"## Task Overview

Verify that README.md only references DCG and does not mention the old git_safety_guard system.

## Current State (Already Verified)

Based on investigation, README.md:
- Does NOT contain 'git_safety_guard'
- Does NOT contain 'Git Safety Guard' (as a specific tool name)
- DOES contain 'safety guard' generically in contexts like 'safety guardrails via SLB'
- DOES have a comprehensive DCG section (lines 1945-2010)

## Verification Commands

```bash
# Check for specific old tool name
grep -n 'git_safety_guard' README.md  # Should return nothing

# Check for old display name (case-insensitive)
grep -ni 'git safety guard' README.md  # Should return nothing

# Confirm DCG section exists
grep -n 'Destructive Command Guard' README.md  # Should show DCG docs
```

## README.md DCG Section (Lines 1945-2010)

README.md already has comprehensive DCG documentation covering:
- Why DCG exists (the December 17, 2025 incident)
- What gets blocked (reset, checkout --, force push, clean, branch -D, stash, rm -rf)
- What gets allowed (safe variants like checkout -b, restore --staged)
- Installation command
- Claude Code configuration
- Modular pack system

## Status

This task is essentially a verification that no action is needed. The README was properly updated when DCG was introduced.

## Notes

Generic 'safety guard' or 'guardrails' references in README.md are fine - they refer to the concept, not the old tool specifically. Examples that are OK:
- 'Safety guardrails via SLB' (referring to general safety mechanisms)
- 'safety guards' in flywheel description
- DCG's own description as a 'guard'","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":10,"created_at":"2026-01-24T20:38:57.589145554Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T20:38:57.589145554Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["documentation","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33vh.7","depends_on_id":"bd-33vh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-24T20:38:57.589145554Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-33vh.8","title":"End-to-end test: Fresh install + doctor confirms DCG, no git_safety_guard warnings","description":"## Task Overview

Run a complete end-to-end test to verify:
1. Fresh ACFS installation doesn't create git_safety_guard artifacts
2. `acfs doctor` shows DCG check, NOT git_safety_guard check
3. No warnings about missing git_safety_guard files

## Test Environment

Use the existing Docker-based test harness:
```bash
./tests/vm/test_install_ubuntu.sh
```

## Test Cases

### Test Case 1: Fresh Install Verification
```bash
# After fresh install in Docker
ls -la ~/.acfs/claude/              # Should only show settings.json
ls -la ~/.acfs/claude/hooks/ 2>&1   # Should fail (directory doesn't exist)
ls -la ~/.claude/hooks/ 2>&1        # Should fail or be empty (DCG doesn't use this)
```

### Test Case 2: Doctor Output Verification
```bash
acfs doctor 2>&1 | tee doctor_output.txt

# Should NOT contain
grep -i 'git.safety.guard' doctor_output.txt && echo 'FAIL: Found git_safety_guard' || echo 'PASS: No git_safety_guard'

# Should contain DCG check
grep -i 'DCG' doctor_output.txt && echo 'PASS: Found DCG check' || echo 'FAIL: Missing DCG check'
```

### Test Case 3: Install Log Verification
```bash
# During install, check log output
# Should see: 'Installing DCG' or similar
# Should NOT see: 'Git Safety Guard'
```

### Test Case 4: Settings.json Verification
```bash
cat ~/.claude/settings.json 2>/dev/null || cat ~/.config/claude/settings.json

# Should NOT contain 'git_safety_guard'
# May or may not contain 'dcg' (depends on whether dcg install was run)
```

## Expected Results

| Check | Expected |
|-------|----------|
| ~/.acfs/claude/hooks/ exists | NO |
| ~/.claude/hooks/git_safety_guard.py exists | NO |
| acfs doctor mentions 'Git safety guard' | NO |
| acfs doctor mentions 'DCG' | YES |
| install.sh log mentions 'Git Safety Guard' | NO (after bd-33vh.1 fix) |
| install.sh log mentions 'DCG' | YES (after bd-33vh.1 fix) |

## Integration with CI

This test should be added to or verified by:
- `.github/workflows/installer.yml` - Docker integration tests
- `.github/workflows/installer-canary.yml` - Daily canary runs

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Docker-based fresh install completes successfully
2. No git_safety_guard warnings in doctor output
3. DCG check present and either passes or gives appropriate guidance
4. Install logs use 'DCG' terminology
5. No stale artifacts created","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":45,"created_at":"2026-01-24T20:39:16.986042720Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T20:40:11.715737284Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","qa","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33vh.8","depends_on_id":"bd-33vh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-24T20:39:16.986042720Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-33vh.8","depends_on_id":"bd-33vh.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-24T20:40:06.307562642Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-33vh.8","depends_on_id":"bd-33vh.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-24T20:40:09.087861241Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-33vh.8","depends_on_id":"bd-33vh.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-24T20:40:11.715678744Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Run a complete end-to-end test to verify complete git_safety_guard removal with DETAILED LOGGING.

## Test Script Location

Create: tests/e2e/test_git_safety_guard_removal.sh

## Logging Requirements

ALL tests MUST:
1. Log to timestamped file: /tmp/git_safety_guard_removal_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).log
2. Structured format: [TIMESTAMP] [LEVEL] [TEST_NAME] Message
3. JSON results file

## Test Environment

Use Docker-based test harness: ./tests/vm/test_install_ubuntu.sh

## Test Cases

### Test Case 1: Fresh Install Verification
```bash
# After fresh install in Docker
ls -la ~/.acfs/claude/              # Should only show settings.json
ls -la ~/.acfs/claude/hooks/ 2>&1   # Should fail (directory doesn't exist)
ls -la ~/.claude/hooks/ 2>&1        # Should fail or be empty
```

### Test Case 2: Doctor Output Verification
```bash
acfs doctor 2>&1 | tee doctor_output.txt

# Should NOT contain
! grep -i 'git.safety.guard' doctor_output.txt

# Should contain DCG check
grep -i 'DCG' doctor_output.txt
```

### Test Case 3: Install Log Verification
- Should see: 'Installing DCG' or similar
- Should NOT see: 'Git Safety Guard'

### Test Case 4: Settings.json Verification
```bash
cat ~/.claude/settings.json 2>/dev/null
# Should NOT contain 'git_safety_guard'
```

### Test Case 5: Codebase Audit
```bash
# No git_safety_guard in codebase
! grep -ri 'git_safety_guard' --include='*.sh' --include='*.ts' --include='*.md' .
```

## Expected Results Table

| Check | Expected |
|-------|----------|
| ~/.acfs/claude/hooks/ exists | NO |
| ~/.claude/hooks/git_safety_guard.py exists | NO |
| acfs doctor mentions 'Git safety guard' | NO |
| acfs doctor mentions 'DCG' | YES |
| install.sh log mentions 'Git Safety Guard' | NO |
| install.sh log mentions 'DCG' | YES |

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Docker-based fresh install completes successfully
2. No git_safety_guard warnings in doctor output
3. DCG check present and passes
4. Install logs use 'DCG' terminology
5. No stale artifacts created
6. Detailed timestamped log file generated
7.  {"id":"bd-33vh.9","title":"Document migration path for existing users in CHANGELOG or release notes","description":"## Task Overview

Document the git_safety_guard → DCG migration for users who may be upgrading from older ACFS installations.

## Target Audience

Users who installed ACFS before January 11, 2026 (commit f1fd501) and may:
1. See 'Git safety guard' warnings in acfs doctor
2. Have stale git_safety_guard.py files
3. Be confused about the difference between the old and new systems

## Documentation Locations

### Option 1: Add to CHANGELOG.md (if exists)
```markdown
## [0.x.x] - 2026-01-xx

### Changed
- **BREAKING**: git_safety_guard replaced by DCG (Destructive Command Guard)

### Migration
If you see 'Git safety guard' warnings after updating:
1. Run `acfs update` to get the latest scripts
2. Remove legacy files: `rm -f ~/.acfs/claude/hooks/git_safety_guard.py`
3. Install DCG: `dcg install`
4. Verify: `acfs doctor` should show DCG check, not git_safety_guard
```

### Option 2: Add migration section to README.md
```markdown
## Upgrading from Pre-January 2026 Installations

If you installed ACFS before January 11, 2026, you may have the old git_safety_guard system.
The replacement is DCG (Destructive Command Guard).

### Symptoms of Old Installation
- `acfs doctor` shows 'Git safety guard' warning
- File exists: ~/.acfs/claude/hooks/git_safety_guard.py

### Migration Steps
1. Update ACFS: `acfs update --force`
2. Install DCG: `dcg install`
3. Verify: `acfs doctor` should show only DCG

The update process automatically cleans up legacy git_safety_guard files.
```

### Option 3: In-tool messaging (services-setup.sh)
When user runs `acfs services-setup`, if legacy files detected:
```
⚠️  Legacy git_safety_guard detected
   This has been replaced by DCG (Destructive Command Guard).
   
   Cleaning up old files...
   ✓ Removed ~/.acfs/claude/hooks/git_safety_guard.py
   
   To configure DCG, select 'DCG' from the menu or run: dcg install
```

## Recommended Approach

Combine Options 1 and 3:
1. Document in CHANGELOG for release notes
2. Add proactive cleanup messaging in services-setup.sh

## Content Requirements

Documentation should explain:
1. **What changed**: git_safety_guard.py removed, DCG introduced
2. **Why**: Better performance (Rust vs Python), modular packs, dedicated maintenance
3. **How to migrate**: Run acfs update, install dcg
4. **How to verify**: acfs doctor shows DCG, no git_safety_guard warnings
5. **Timeline**: When this changed (January 11, 2026)

## Notes

- Keep migration docs concise - most users won't need them
- Focus on actionable steps, not history
- Link to DCG section in README for full documentation","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":15,"created_at":"2026-01-24T20:39:38.244493576Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T20:40:03.529994307Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["documentation","migration","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33vh.9","depends_on_id":"bd-33vh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-24T20:39:38.244493576Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-33vh.9","depends_on_id":"bd-33vh.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-24T20:40:03.529936278Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-34mf","title":"Optimize plan/list/print/dry-run fast paths","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T19:00:29.646180400Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T19:21:27.575225697Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T19:21:27.575181604Z","close_reason":"Fast path optimization already implemented: source_generated_installers is skipped when running --list-modules, --print-plan, --dry-run, or --print modes. This avoids unnecessary script sourcing and initialization for operations that only need to display information.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-34mf","depends_on_id":"bd-2z32","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-01-21T19:00:29.676141538Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3fd3","title":"JFP: Switch installer to official CLI + checksums","description":"Align JFP install with official CLI script and checksum verification.\n\nScope:\n- Update acfs.manifest.yaml stack.jeffreysprompts to use verified_installer with official install CLI (https://jeffreysprompts.com/install-cli.sh).\n- Add checksums.yaml entry for jfp installer (compute sha256 via scripts/lib/security.sh --checksum).\n- Regenerate scripts if needed (packages/manifest).\n\nValidation:\n- bun run generate:validate (packages/manifest) if convenient; otherwise note not run.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T09:45:44.088710307Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T09:52:51.548924906Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T09:52:51.548479497Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
